feed.title: Pronounce — recent dictionary additions
feed.subtitle: How engineers actually pronounce project / product / jargon names. Community-maintained.

entry.title: prefix-tuning — pree fix tooning
entry.summary: prefix-tuning is pronounced "pree fix tooning" (/ˈpriːfɪks ˌtjuːnɪŋ/). Tune a learned prefix prepended to each layer's keys/values.

entry.title: instruct — in strukt
entry.summary: instruct is pronounced "in strukt" (/ɪnˈstrʌkt/). "in-STRUKT" — short for instruction-tuned (GPT-3.5 Instruct, Llama-2-Instruct, etc.).

entry.title: few-shot — few shot
entry.summary: few-shot is pronounced "few shot" (/ˌfjuː ˈʃɒt/). "FEW-shot" — solve a task from k examples in the prompt; no weight updates.

entry.title: zero-shot — zee roh shot
entry.summary: zero-shot is pronounced "zee roh shot" (/ˌzɪroʊ ˈʃɒt/). "ZEE-roh-shot" — solve a task with no in-context examples.

entry.title: transfer-learning — trans fer ler ning
entry.summary: transfer-learning is pronounced "trans fer ler ning" (/ˈtrænsfər ˌlɜːrnɪŋ/). Reuse representations learned on one task for another; the parent concept of fine-tuning.

entry.title: continual-learning — kun tin yoo ul ler ning
entry.summary: continual-learning is pronounced "kun tin yoo ul ler ning" (/kənˈtɪnjuəl ˌlɜːrnɪŋ/). Sequential training across tasks without catastrophic forgetting; "kun-TIN-yoo-ul learning".

entry.title: curriculum-learning — kuh rik yu lum ler ning
entry.summary: curriculum-learning is pronounced "kuh rik yu lum ler ning" (/kəˈrɪkjələm ˌlɜːrnɪŋ/). Train on easy examples first, then harder; "kuh-RIK-yu-lum learning".

entry.title: scaling-laws — skay ling lawz
entry.summary: scaling-laws is pronounced "skay ling lawz" (/ˈskeɪlɪŋ ˌlɔːz/). Power-law relations between compute / data / params and loss; "SKAY-ling laws".

entry.title: downstream — down streem
entry.summary: downstream is pronounced "down streem" (/ˌdaʊnˈstriːm/). "DOWN-STREEM" — the application/eval task that a pretrained model is adapted to.

entry.title: alignment — uh lyne munt
entry.summary: alignment is pronounced "uh lyne munt" (/əˈlaɪnmənt/). "uh-LYNE-munt" — making model behavior match human intent and values.

entry.title: jailbreak — jayl brake
entry.summary: jailbreak is pronounced "jayl brake" (/ˈdʒeɪlˌbreɪk/). "JAYL-brake" — prompt or attack that bypasses an LM's safety policies.

entry.title: red-teaming — red teeming
entry.summary: red-teaming is pronounced "red teeming" (/ˈrɛd ˌtiːmɪŋ/). "RED-teeming" — adversarial probing of an LM to find unsafe outputs.

entry.title: reward-model — rih ward mod ul
entry.summary: reward-model is pronounced "rih ward mod ul" (/rɪˈwɔːrd ˌmɒdəl/). RM in RLHF — scores responses to drive PPO/DPO; "rih-WARD model".

entry.title: preference — pref er uns
entry.summary: preference is pronounced "pref er uns" (/ˈprɛfərəns/). "PREF-er-uns" — pairwise (A > B) judgements collected for preference learning.

entry.title: HHH — aitch aitch aitch
entry.summary: HHH is pronounced "aitch aitch aitch" (/ˌeɪtʃ eɪtʃ ˈeɪtʃ/). Helpful, Honest, Harmless — Anthropic's alignment goal triad; letters "H-H-H".

entry.title: grounding — grown ding
entry.summary: grounding is pronounced "grown ding" (/ˈɡraʊndɪŋ/). "GROWN-ding" — tying generations to verifiable evidence (retrieved docs, tool output).

entry.title: hallucination — huh loo sih nay shun
entry.summary: hallucination is pronounced "huh loo sih nay shun" (/həˌluːsɪˈneɪʃən/). "huh-LOO-sih-NAY-shun" — an LM's confident but ungrounded fabrications.

entry.title: hallucinate — huh loo sih nate
entry.summary: hallucinate is pronounced "huh loo sih nate" (/həˈluːsɪˌneɪt/). "huh-LOO-sih-nate" — verb form.

entry.title: agent — ay junt
entry.summary: agent is pronounced "ay junt" (/ˈeɪdʒənt/). "AY-junt" — an LM-driven loop that perceives, plans, calls tools, and acts.

entry.title: agentic — ay jen tik
entry.summary: agentic is pronounced "ay jen tik" (/eɪˈdʒɛntɪk/). "ay-JEN-tik" — adjective; capable of multi-step autonomous action.

entry.title: tool-use — tool yoos
entry.summary: tool-use is pronounced "tool yoos" (/ˈtuːl ˌjuːs/). "TOOL-YOOS" — LM-invoked external functions (search, code exec, calc).

entry.title: function-calling — funk shun call ing
entry.summary: function-calling is pronounced "funk shun call ing" (/ˈfʌŋkʃən ˌkɔːlɪŋ/). OpenAI/JSON-schema-style tool invocation API; "FUNK-shun calling".

entry.title: prompt — prompt
entry.summary: prompt is pronounced "prompt" (/prɒmpt/). "PROMPT" — the input string fed to an LM.

entry.title: prompting — prompt ing
entry.summary: prompting is pronounced "prompt ing" (/ˈprɒmptɪŋ/). "PROMPT-ing" — the noun form.

entry.title: reasoning — ree zun ing
entry.summary: reasoning is pronounced "ree zun ing" (/ˈriːzənɪŋ/). "REE-zun-ing" — multi-step inference; also the name of the model class (o1, R1, etc.).
